title: Rich Smoky Chinese Pork Dumplings
description: A smoky and savory Chinese dish featuring pork dumplings cooked in a rich and flavorful sauce, perfect for a weeknight dinner. The use of white wine vinegar and render-fat adds a depth of flavor to the dish.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the ground pork, grated ginger, minced garlic, chopped green onions, salt, and black pepper, and mix well until just combined.
Add the white wine vinegar and render-fat to the pork mixture, and mix until the ingredients are just combined, being careful not to overmix.
Lay a dumpling wrapper on a clean surface, and place a tablespoon of the pork mixture in the center of the wrapper.
Dip your finger in a small amount of water, and run it along the edge of the dumpling wrapper, then fold the wrapper into a triangle by bringing the two opposite corners together to form a point.
Press the edges of the dumpling together to seal the filling inside, and repeat with the remaining dumpling wrappers and filling.
Heat the sesame oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat, then add the dumplings to the skillet, being careful not to overcrowd.
Cook the dumplings for 2-3 minutes on each side, until they are golden brown and crispy on the bottom.
Add 1/4 cup of water to the skillet, then cover with a lid and steam the dumplings for an additional 5 minutes, until they are cooked through and the filling is no longer pink.
Serve the dumplings hot with your favorite dipping sauce.
